text: Scientists can now cut DNA like text — and this is how CRISPR works. Meet CRISPR-Cas9, a revolutionary tool that can edit genes with precision. It uses a guide RNA to find the exact spot in the DNA sequence. Then, Cas9 acts like molecular scissors, cutting the DNA. This allows cells to repair the DNA, potentially eliminating diseases. But remember, clinical use is tightly regulated. Intrigued by CRISPR? Hit like and follow for more science insights!
